transcript: 1984's Ghostbusters follows parapsychology professors Peter Venkman, Ray Stantz, and Egon Spengler, who begin investigating paranormal activity around New York City. After a run-in with a ghost at the New York Public Library, the three are fired from their jobs and decide to start their business, Ghostbusters, in which they would rid the city of its ghosts. They develop a technology to trap the ghosts, set up shop in an old, unused firehouse, and hire a secretary named Janine Melnitz. While business for the Ghostbusters is initially slow, things pick up considerably when they capture a ghost named Slimer and the story is picked up by the media. Soon, business is booming and the Ghostbusters become local celebrities. To help out with the newfound demand, they hire on a fourth Ghostbuster, Winston Zedmore. The Ghostbusters get a call from Dana Barrett, who encounters a demonic dog in her apartment that calls out the name Zool. Vinkman takes a liking to Dana and agrees to help her. Unfortunately, Dana becomes possessed by Zool, known as the Gatekeeper, and Dana's quirky neighbor Louis Tully is possessed by Vins Clortho, the Keymaster. If brought together, they would summon Gozer Gazarian, a god of destruction. And if that wasn't enough trouble for the Ghostbusters, they also had to deal with Walter I'll see you next time. Otho, the Keymaster. If brought together, they would summon Gozer Gazarian, a god of destruction. And if that wasn't enough trouble for the Ghostbusters, they also had to deal with Walter Peck, an investigator for the Environmental Protection Agency who believed that their ghost containment technology was dangerous. Peck has the Ghostbusters containment unit shut off, which causes an explosion that releases all of their previously captured ghosts. In the chaos, Zuul and Vins open the gate between dimensions at Dana's apartment to summon Gozer. The Ghostbusters arrive to defeat the evil god, who ask which form they'd like their destruction to take. Rey accidentally thinks of the Stay Puft Marshmallow Man, and so that is the form Gozer takes to destroy the city. The only way to defeat the giant villain was to cross the energy streams of their proton packs, something that Egon had strongly advised against as too dangerous previously. Luckily, crossing the streams works, and Gozer was defeated and the gateway was closed. Dana and Louis were no longer possessed, Dana and Veekman share a kiss, and the city was saved, leading to crowds rallying around the Ghostbusters as heroes. In Ghostbusters 2, five years have passed since the team saved the world from Gozer. After the city sued them for the immense property damage caused by the Stay Puft Marshmallow Man, the company has gone out of business. Rey now owns an occult bookstore and is an unsuccessful children's birthday party entertainer, with Winston on the side. Egon is back to running scientific experiments in the lab, and Vinkman is the host of a talk show about psychics. Vinkman and Dana broke up years ago, and in the years since, she's become a single mother to Oscar and taken a museum job cleaning artwork. At the museum, a painting of Viggo the Carpathian comes to life and possesses Dana's boss, Janis. Viggo wants Janis to bring him a baby to possess so he can be reborn and conquer the world. And because Janis is a little obsessed with Dana, he decides to go after Oscar. Dana turns to the Ghostbusters for their help and they discover a river of slime running under the city. While investigating the slime, they accidentally break a power line which causes all of New York City to go dark. The Ghostbusters are forced into court for the damages where Lewis Tully serves as their inept lawyer, but the slime explodes and summons Ghost to the courtroom. The Ghostbusters capture the ghost and are cleared of all charges. The event causes a resurgence in the Ghostbusters' popularity and they return to business. And what would the Ghostbusters be without their trusty secretary Janine returning, who has started her own romantic relationship with wannabe Ghostbuster Lewis. As Venkman and Dana rekindle their relationship, Ray, Egon, and Winston discover that the slime river leads directly to the museum and that it has amassed the negative emotions of all New Yorkers. If the slime gets on you, you start to become influenced by the slime's negative energy. When Janusz kidnaps Oscar and takes him to the museum for Viggo to possess, the Ghostbusters realize they need the slime to harness positive emotions in order to stop the tyrant. And so, they douse the Statue of Liberty in slime and bring it to life, then ride it through the streets as a symbol of positivity. The plan works, and the city rallies around the Ghostbusters, with the suited-up new Ghostbuster Lewis leading a chorus of singing. The Ghostbusters, aided by the power of positive slime, are able to enter the museum to defeat Vigo and destroy his painting. The team had once again saved the day and were lauded by the city as heroes. And that's everything that happened in the first two Ghostbusters movies. Let me know some of your favorite moments from the movies in the comments below. Let me know what you're most looking forward to seeing in Ghostbusters Afterlife. Are you excited that the old gang is coming back for this new adventure? Like this video if you found it helpful. Subscribe to this channel for more content like it. And that does it for me in this video, but I'll see you in the next one.
